args.name: offer_draft
args.content:
[Analytics Dojo SMC Private Letterhead]

[Date]

Sara Khan  
[Address Line 1]  
[Address Line 2]  
[City], [Postal Code]  
Pakistan

Subject: Offer of Employment – Data Analyst

Dear Sara Khan,

Analytics Dojo SMC Private ("ADS", "we", "us" or "the Company") is pleased to offer you employment in the position of Data Analyst (the "Position"), on the terms and conditions set out in this letter (the "Offer Letter"). This Offer Letter is governed by, and shall be interpreted in accordance with, the laws of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan, subject to all applicable labour and employment laws.

1. Position and Reporting
You will be employed as a Data Analyst, based in [BLANK LOCATION]. You will report to [BLANK REPORTING MANAGER NAME AND TITLE] or such other person as the Company may designate from time to time. Your duties and responsibilities will be as assigned by the Company and may be updated in line with the Company’s business needs, provided that they remain consistent with the Position and applicable law.

2. Start Date and Work Schedule
Your employment with the Company is expected to commence on [BLANK START DATE] (the "Start Date"), subject to completion of all pre-employment formalities required by the Company and applicable law.

Your normal working hours will be [BLANK STANDARD WORKING HOURS, E.G., 9:00 A.M. TO 6:00 P.M., MONDAY TO FRIDAY], with a total of [BLANK] hours per week, inclusive of rest and prayer breaks as required by law and Company policy. You agree to work such additional hours as may reasonably be required for the proper performance of your duties, in accordance with applicable labour laws on working time and overtime.

3. Employment Status and Probation
Your employment will be on a full-time basis. You will initially be engaged on a probationary period of [BLANK PROBATION PERIOD, E.G., THREE (3) MONTHS] commencing from the Start Date, in accordance with applicable Pakistani labour law. During the probationary period, your performance and suitability for the Position will be assessed. The Company may, in its discretion and subject to law, confirm your employment, extend the probationary period, or terminate your employment by providing the notice (or payment in lieu of notice) required under applicable law and Company policy.

Upon successful completion of probation and confirmation in writing by the Company, you will be considered a regular employee, subject to the terms of this Offer Letter, the Company’s policies, and applicable law.

4. Compensation
Your gross base salary will be [BLANK BASE SALARY AMOUNT IN PKR] per [BLANK PAY PERIOD, E.G., MONTH], payable in arrears in accordance with the Company’s standard payroll practices and subject to applicable tax and statutory deductions.

You may be eligible for such bonuses, incentives, or variable compensation as the Company may establish from time to time for similarly situated employees. Any such bonus or incentive schemes are discretionary, may be amended or withdrawn by the Company at any time, and do not form part of your guaranteed compensation.

5. Benefits and Statutory Entitlements
You will be entitled to participate in the employee benefit plans and programs that the Company may make available to employees in Pakistan from time to time, subject to the terms of those plans and applicable eligibility requirements. These may include, without limitation, health insurance, provident fund, gratuity, and other benefits as determined by the Company in its discretion and in compliance with applicable law.

You will be entitled to annual leave, casual leave, sick leave, public holidays, and any other statutory leave in accordance with applicable Pakistani labour laws and the Company’s leave policies as amended from time to time.

6. Taxes and Deductions
All amounts payable to you under this Offer Letter will be subject to applicable income tax, social security contributions, and any other statutory deductions required under the laws of Pakistan. The Company will withhold and remit such amounts as required by law.

7. Place of Work and Remote/Hybrid Work
Your primary place of work will be the Company’s office located at [BLANK OFFICE ADDRESS], or such other location within Pakistan as the Company may reasonably require, subject to applicable law. The Company may, at its discretion, permit remote or hybrid working arrangements in accordance with its policies, which may be amended from time to time.

8. Confidentiality and Intellectual Property
As a condition of your employment, you will be required to comply with the Company’s confidentiality, data protection, and information security policies. You must not, during or after your employment, disclose to any third party or use for your own benefit any confidential or proprietary information of the Company, its clients, or business partners, except as required in the proper performance of your duties or as required by law.

You acknowledge that any and all work product, data, analyses, software, documentation, inventions, discoveries, improvements, designs, reports, and other materials created, developed, or contributed to by you, alone or with others, in the course of your employment or using the Company’s resources ("Work Product") shall be the sole and exclusive property of the Company to the fullest extent permitted by law. You hereby assign to the Company all rights, title, and interest in and to such Work Product, including all intellectual property rights, and agree to execute any documents and do all acts reasonably required to give effect to this provision.

9. Company Policies and Code of Conduct
You agree to comply with all Company policies, procedures, and codes of conduct in force from time to time, including but not limited to policies on confidentiality, data protection, information security, anti-harassment, anti-discrimination, anti-bribery and corruption, health and safety, use of Company assets, and social media. The Company may amend or introduce new policies at its discretion, provided that such policies are consistent with applicable law.

10. Working Hours, Overtime, and Rest Days
Your working hours, overtime, and rest days will be governed by applicable Pakistani labour laws and the Company’s policies. Where overtime is required, it will be compensated or otherwise managed in accordance with applicable law and Company policy.

11. Termination of Employment
Following the probationary period, either you or the Company may terminate your employment by giving the other party not less than [BLANK NOTICE PERIOD, E.G., THIRTY (30) DAYS] prior written notice or payment in lieu of notice, in accordance with applicable Pakistani labour laws and Company policy.

The Company may terminate your employment without notice or payment in lieu of notice in the event of gross misconduct, willful disobedience, fraud, dishonesty, or any other grounds recognized under applicable law as justifying summary dismissal.

Upon termination of your employment for any reason, you must immediately return to the Company all Company property, including but not limited to equipment, documents, records, data, and any copies thereof, and you must comply with all exit formalities prescribed by the Company.

12. Non-Solicitation and Conflict of Interest
During your employment and for a period of [BLANK NON-SOLICITATION PERIOD, E.G., SIX (6) MONTHS] following the termination of your employment, you agree that you will not, directly or indirectly, solicit or attempt to solicit any employee, consultant, or key contractor of the Company to terminate their relationship with the Company, nor will you induce or attempt to induce any client or key business partner of the Company to reduce or terminate its business with the Company, to the extent permitted by applicable law.

You must promptly disclose to the Company any actual or potential conflict of interest that may arise in connection with your employment.

13. Data Protection and Privacy
The Company will process your personal data in accordance with applicable data protection laws and the Company’s data protection and privacy policies. By accepting this Offer Letter, you consent to the collection, use, processing, transfer, and storage of your personal data by the Company and its affiliates for legitimate business and employment-related purposes, subject to applicable law.

14. Entire Agreement and Amendments
This Offer Letter, together with any documents expressly referred to in it (including Company policies and any separate confidentiality or intellectual property agreements you may be required to sign), constitutes the entire agreement between you and the Company with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior discussions, understandings, or offers, whether written or oral, relating to your employment with the Company.

Any amendment or modification to this Offer Letter will be valid only if made in writing and signed by an authorized representative of the Company.

15. Governing Law and Dispute Resolution
This Offer Letter and any dispute arising out of or in connection with it or your employment with the Company shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan. Any disputes shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of [BLANK CITY/PROVINCE IN PAKISTAN], without prejudice to any mandatory dispute resolution mechanisms provided under applicable labour laws.

16. Conditions Precedent
This offer of employment is conditional upon:
(a) your provision of accurate and complete information in your job application and during the recruitment process;
(b) your ability to demonstrate your legal right to work in Pakistan and to perform the Position; and
(c) satisfactory completion of any background, reference, or credential checks that the Company may reasonably require, in compliance with applicable law.

If any of the above conditions are not satisfied, or if any information provided by you is found to be materially false or misleading, the Company may withdraw this offer or terminate your employment, subject to applicable law.

17. Acceptance of Offer
Please indicate your acceptance of this Offer Letter and the terms and conditions contained herein by signing and dating the acknowledgment below and returning a scanned copy to [BLANK COMPANY CONTACT EMAIL] by no later than [BLANK ACCEPTANCE DEADLINE DATE]. If you do not accept this offer by that date, it will automatically lapse, unless otherwise agreed in writing by the Company.

We look forward to you joining Analytics Dojo SMC Private and contributing to our mission.

Yours sincerely,

For and on behalf of  
Analytics Dojo SMC Private


______________________________  
[BLANK AUTHORIZED SIGNATORY NAME]  
[BLANK AUTHORIZED SIGNATORY TITLE]


Acknowledgment and Acceptance

I, Sara Khan, hereby acknowledge that I have read and understood the terms and conditions of this Offer Letter and accept employment with Analytics Dojo SMC Private on the terms set out above.

______________________________  
Sara Khan  
Date: ________________________


Attorney-Review Disclaimer

This draft employment offer letter has been prepared by an AI assistant based on standard Analytics Dojo SMC Private (ADS) defaults and general principles of Pakistani labour law as of the date of drafting. It does not constitute legal advice, does not create a lawyer–client relationship, and may not reflect the most current legal or regulatory developments. The contents of this document should be reviewed, customized, and approved by a qualified attorney licensed in the relevant jurisdiction before being used, signed, or relied upon. ADS and the AI provider disclaim any responsibility or liability for actions taken or not taken based on this draft without appropriate independent legal advice.
